div1.entryFree:
Clout, 1) piece of cloth or linen: or madly think a babe of --s were he, John III, 4, 58. gavest the duke a c. steeped in . . . R3 I, 3, 177. she looks as pale as any c. Rom. II, 4, 218. a c. upon that head where late the diadem stood, Hml. II, 2, 529. with --s about their heads, Ant. IV, 7, 6 (or is it here == a cuff?) (cf. Swathing-clouts and Dishclout).
2) the marked centre of the butts: he'll ne'er hit the c. LLL IV, 1, 136. clapped i' the c. H4B III, 2, 51. i' the c.! Lr IV, 6, 92.
